title: xiaoyi-ssg Interactions Manifest Schema
description: Per-site browser-interaction contract. Each entry declares one interaction (search / filter / lightbox / etc.) and the assets it needs. render.js validates this at startup; dev.js watches it; script.js bootstraps the registered interactions at runtime. See prompts/pipeline-generation.md §9.

interactions.description: List of browser interactions. May be empty; the engine injects default mobile-menu + theme-toggle interactions when the array is empty AND the templates emit [data-interaction] elements.

name.description: Stable kebab-case identifier. Used as the key in `data-interaction="<name>"` attributes, as the filename stem for auto-generated data files, and as a fragment of the corresponding `assets/data/<name>.json` path.

pages.description: View names (in `template-manifest.json`) or output URL prefixes this interaction ships on. Empty = site-wide.

selectors.description: CSS selectors (relative to the page) that mark nodes this interaction owns. Default: `[data-interaction="<name>"]`.

modules.description: JS modules to load for this interaction, relative to the site root. Files must exist under `assets/interactions/` or `assets/scripts/` and are copied verbatim to `public/assets/...`.

data.description: Static data files this interaction reads, relative to the site root. Each must be produced by `buildExtras` (see prompts/render-node-spec.md) and included in the per-page render hash.

content_types.description: Source names whose items contribute to this interaction's auto-generated data. Used by `buildExtras` (e.g. a filter's facet counts need to know which sources to aggregate over). Defaults to all sources when absent.

version.description: Exact pinned version (no ranges). Recorded in `interactions.manifest.json` so `npm install` is reproducible.

dependencies.description: Extra npm packages this interaction needs. Pinned exact versions; recorded here for transparency and reproducibility. Default deps (js-yaml / marked / chokidar / eta) are NOT listed — they are dev-time render deps, not browser interaction deps.

fallback.description: What the user sees when JS is disabled or the module fails to load. The page must remain readable / linked without the interaction (e.g. 'full project list remains visible without JavaScript').

accessibility.description: A11y primitives this interaction implements. Engine does not enforce; this is documentation for the AI generator and for the eventual accessibility audit.

verification.description: Manual verification steps (used by the AI when reporting success to the user, and by the eventual human reviewer).
